paper.title: Betulinic Acid Suppresses UBE2T Expression via MAPK/ERK Inhibition to Block FANCI and FANCD2 Monoubiquitination in Glioblastoma

author_details.name: Yifeng Bao
affiliations: The First Affiliated Hospital of Xi'an Jiaotong University

author_details.name: Maode Wang
affiliations: The First Affiliated Hospital of Xi'an Jiaotong University
The First Affiliated Hospital of Xi'an Jiaotong University

entities.name: The First Affiliated Hospital of Xi'an Jiaotong University
Betulinic Acid Suppresses UBE2T Expression via MAPK/ERK Inhibition to Block FANCI and FANCD2 Monoubiquitination in Glioblastoma

evidence_entities.text: BA pretreatment abrogated cisplatin‐induced monoubiquitination of FANCI/FANCD2 and disrupted their nuclear foci formation
BA selectively suppressed UBE2T expression at the transcriptional level
BA inhibited MAPK/ERK signalling, and pharmacological reactivation of ERK reversed BA‐induced suppression of UBE2T
when BA was administered prior to cisplatin treatment, this modification of FANCI and FANCD2 was largely abrogated
cells pretreated with BA displayed a pronounced defect in the resolution of interstrand crosslinks
only UBE2T exhibited a pronounced reduction in protein abundance when glioma cells were pretreated with BA
Animals receiving combined treatment with cisplatin and BA displayed a pronounced reduction in tumour growth
